title: Neurolaena lobata
taxonomy: Neurolaena lobata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Neurolaena. The plant is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Neurolaena lobata is also known as Mexican daisy or yellow daisy tree. Its classification is further detailed under the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Neurolaena lobata is known for its traditional medicinal uses, particularly in Latin American folklore. The plant is reported to have anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. In some regions, it is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Furthermore, Neurolaena lobata is believed to have antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and preventing infections. However, more scientific research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.

intro: Neurolaena lobata, also known as yerba de San Juan, is a perennial shrub native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, valued for its medicinal and ornamental properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Neurolaena lobata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Neurolaena lobata is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to alleviate symptoms of coughs, colds, and other respiratory afflictions.
